compiled_state.title: ticket-transition.sh: open-children scan over full ledger -> O(N) subprocess invocations cause 15-30s transition times at scale
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
ticket-transition.sh's open-children guard (Step 1b) scans ALL ticket directories in .tickets-tracker/ to find open children of the target ticket. For tickets lacking a SNAPSHOT file, it invokes ticket-reducer.py as a subprocess per ticket (slow path). With a large ticket ledger this adds 15-30 seconds per transition.

## Actual Behavior
Closing stories during /dso:sprint Phase 6 (6 stories) took several minutes because each transition scanned the full ledger. When multiple transitions were fired concurrently, ticket-reducer.py processes piled up on the .ticket-write.lock, making transitions appear to hang.

## Expected Behavior
The open-children check should be scoped to the target ticket's known children (via parent_id index or SNAPSHOT fast-path) rather than scanning all tickets. Transitions should complete in under 5 seconds for tickets with SNAPSHOTs.

## Fix Options
Option A: Build a reverse-parent-id index in the SNAPSHOT so open-children lookup is O(1) rather than O(N).
Option B: Pre-filter using ticket list output (already loaded in memory by callers) before invoking the full subprocess scan.
Option C: Enforce serialization in the CLI — reject concurrent transitions with a clear error rather than silently competing on the flock.
